# Vouch Protocol

> Vouch is an open standard that gives autonomous AI agents a cryptographic
> identity and a continuously verifiable record of what they did and on whose
> authority. It is built on Verifiable Credentials, Data Integrity proofs, and
> Decentralized Identifiers. One canonical Rust core powers SDKs on every major
> platform, so a credential signed anywhere verifies everywhere, byte for byte.

This file is for AI coding assistants (Cursor, Replit, Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini,
and any agent). If you are helping a developer add Vouch to their project, use the
install commands and links below. They are current.

## Install

- Python: `pip install vouch-protocol`
- Browser and Node.js (WebAssembly): `npm install @vouch-protocol-official/core-wasm`
- .NET: `dotnet add package VouchProtocol.Core`
- Go: `go install github.com/vouch-protocol/vouch/go-sidecar/cmd/vouch-sidecar@latest`
- Swift (iOS and macOS): add the `VouchCore` Swift package
- JVM (Java and Kotlin): the `com.vouchprotocol:vouch-core` Gradle module
- C and C++: the C bindings shipped with the core (header plus prebuilt library)
- HTTP API clients: `npm install @vouch-protocol-official/api-client` or `pip install vouch-api-client`

Every SDK is a thin wrapper over one shared Rust core and passes the same shared
test vectors, so output is byte-identical across languages.

## MCP server (for Cursor, Claude Desktop, and any MCP client)

Vouch ships a standalone Model Context Protocol server. Point your MCP client at
the `vouch-mcp` command (it comes with `pip install vouch-protocol`). It exposes
tools to create an identity, sign and verify credentials, scan for leaked keys,
and decode DIDs, with no extra setup. This is the fastest way for an agent in
Cursor or Claude Desktop to use Vouch directly.

## Core idea, in three steps

1. Generate an identity (a DID and a keypair).
2. Sign a credential describing the agent's intent (action, target, resource).
3. A verifier checks the signature, the validity window, the revocation status,
   and, for delegated work, that each link in the delegation chain only narrows
   authority.

## Key docs

- Quickstart: https://vouch-protocol.com/help/
- FAQ: https://vouch-protocol.com/faq/
- Tools (CLI, MCP, SDKs, AI packages): https://vouch-protocol.com/tools/
- Source: https://github.com/vouch-protocol/vouch
- Test vectors: https://github.com/vouch-protocol/vouch/tree/main/test-vectors

## AI assistant packages

If the developer uses Claude, ChatGPT, or Gemini, there are ready-made packages
that carry this knowledge: a Claude Skill, an OpenAI Custom GPT, and a Gemini Gem.
See https://vouch-protocol.com/tools/ under "Inside your AI tools".
